creator: _jkrow
caption:
If you only have 3- 4 days to travel, everything matters a little more and there’s not much room for things to go wrong.

I used to think the key was planning everything perfectly, down to every stop, every detail. But the reality is that things don’t always go as planned. Flights get delayed, weather shifts, and plans change.

And when your time is limited, that can throw off your entire trip.

Now I focus less on perfection and more on building in flexibility and thinking about the “what ifs” ahead of time, so I can actually enjoy the experience. 

Save this for your next trip if you’re working with limited PTO.

#AXAtravelinsurance AD

transcription.text: When you only have three or four days off, one thing going wrong can throw off your entire trip, and I have learned that the hard way. And our flight got cancelled. The time we missed our flight to Easter Island, everything suddenly felt extra stressful because when your time is limited, there's not much room for error. I used to think the solution was just to plan everything perfectly. Every hotel, every route, every reservation. The more I travel, the more I realize that even when you plan everything right, things still happen. Flights get delayed, weather changes, and plans shift. Now I focus less on trying to control every detail and more on being prepared for the things I can't control, especially before short trips. That's why I book travel insurance through AXA Travel Insurance. It's not that I expect something to go wrong, it's just that I know that travel doesn't always go perfectly. And having that extra support and peace of mind from AXA makes it a lot easier to actually enjoy the trip while I'm there. Because when your PTO is limited, a little extra preparation makes a huge difference.
